title: bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. yagara
intro: Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. yagara, also known as Japanese papyrus, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. yagara can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use.
